inputs.userGoal: Provision an event-driven data processing pipeline using Azure Container Apps with Dapr Pub/Sub integration, connected to Azure Event Grid for ingestion and Cosmos DB for output storage.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required by Container Apps Environment for centralized log collection and diagnostics. PerGB2018 is the recommended pay-as-you-go tier (WAF-Operational Excellence).
reasoning.tradeoffs: Free tier limited to 500 MB/day and 7-day retention — insufficient for production workloads.

resources.name: cae-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Serverless Container Apps Environment with Consumption workload profile provides auto-scaling and pay-per-use billing. Supports Dapr components natively for Pub/Sub integration (WAF-Cost Optimization, WAF-Performance Efficiency).
reasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption profile has fewer networking features (no UDR, no NAT Gateway) compared to dedicated workload profiles, but simplifies operations and reduces cost for event-driven workloads.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Environment requires Log Analytics workspace customerId and sharedKey for log collection.

reasoning.whyChosen: Event Grid provides serverless event routing with at-least-once delivery and native CloudEvents support. CloudEvents schema ensures interoperability with Dapr Pub/Sub (WAF-Reliability, WAF-Performance Efficiency).
reasoning.tradeoffs: Event Grid Basic SKU lacks private endpoint support. Service Bus offers sessions and ordering but adds cost and complexity. Event Hubs better for high-throughput streaming but overkill for discrete event processing.

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB NoSQL API with serverless capacity mode provides pay-per-request pricing ideal for event-driven, variable workloads. Session consistency balances performance with read-your-writes guarantees (WAF-Cost Optimization, WAF-Reliability).
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless is single-region only and cannot use shared-throughput databases. If multi-region writes or high sustained throughput needed, provisioned throughput would be better.

resources.name: cosmos-datapipeline-prod-001/pipeline-db

reasoning.whyChosen: SQL (NoSQL) database container to hold the processed results collection within the Cosmos DB account.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Child resource of the Cosmos DB account.

resources.name: cosmos-datapipeline-prod-001/pipeline-db/processed-results

reasoning.whyChosen: Container to store processed pipeline output data. Partition key on /partitionKey allows flexible partitioning based on workload.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Child resource of the SQL database.

resources.name: ca-processor-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Container App with Dapr sidecar subscribes to Event Grid events via Dapr Pub/Sub component, processes data, and writes results to Cosmos DB. Scale-to-zero minimizes cost when idle. Internal ingress keeps the app private (WAF-Security, WAF-Cost Optimization, WAF-Performance Efficiency).
alternativesConsidered: Azure Functions with Event Grid trigger
reasoning.tradeoffs: Functions would be simpler for single-event processing but Container Apps provides more control over runtime, dependencies, and scaling configuration. AKS adds cluster management overhead.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Container App runs in the environment, subscribes to Event Grid via Dapr Pub/Sub, and writes processed output to Cosmos DB.

resources.name: cae-datapipeline-prod-001/evgt-pubsub

reasoning.whyChosen: Dapr Pub/Sub component for Azure Event Grid provides decoupled event delivery to the processor Container App. Scoped to only the processor app for least-privilege access (WAF-Security).
alternativesConsidered: Direct Event Grid subscription webhook to Container App
reasoning.tradeoffs: Direct webhook avoids Dapr dependency but loses Pub/Sub abstraction and resiliency features. Service Bus adds cost for message queuing capabilities not needed here.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Dapr component is a child of the managed environment and connects to the Event Grid topic.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture uses Azure Container Apps with Dapr Pub/Sub for event-driven processing. Event Grid serves as the event ingestion layer with CloudEvents schema for interoperability. The processor Container App subscribes to events via a Dapr Pub/Sub component, scales from zero based on demand, and writes processed results to a serverless Cosmos DB account. Log Analytics provides centralized observability. All resources are deployed to eastus in a single resource group with Consumption-tier billing to minimize cost.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Prioritized simplicity and cost efficiency over advanced networking (no VNet integration) and multi-region redundancy. Cosmos DB serverless is single-region only. Event Grid Basic SKU lacks private endpoints. For production hardening, consider adding managed identity-based auth (disabling key access), VNet integration with workload profiles, and Application Insights for distributed tracing.

plan.validation: All resources deployed to eastus for co-location. Container Apps Environment references Log Analytics for logging. Container App references the environment via environmentId. Dapr Pub/Sub component is scoped to the processor app and connects to Event Grid topic. Cosmos DB uses serverless + Session consistency (valid single-region combination per pairing checks). CPU/memory combination (0.5 cores / 1Gi) is valid for Consumption workload profile.
